result.summary: Cloud Deploy exposes a Google Cloud REST API at `clouddeploy.googleapis.com` and is also operated via the `gcloud deploy` CLI; both use Google Cloud authentication.
result.description: Google Cloud Deploy is a managed continuous delivery service for promoting application releases across target environments such as GKE and Cloud Run. It lets teams define delivery pipelines, create releases, and manage rollouts, approvals, and automation.

gcp_oauth.label: Google Cloud access token
gcp_oauth.setup: For the easiest path, authenticate with the Google Cloud CLI: install [`gcloud`](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/install-sdk), then run [`gcloud auth login`](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/auth/login) for user credentials or [`gcloud auth activate-service-account`](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/auth/activate-service-account) for a service account. For application-default credentials, use [`gcloud auth application-default login`](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/auth/application-default/login). Use the resulting OAuth 2.0 access token with Google Cloud APIs.
